mechanism_narrative: A downward drift in FEDFUNDS signals easier discount rates and reduced financial stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening labor income and lower household spending, which depresses equity earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, the economy is losing retail and housing demand simultaneously, a combination that should reduce earnings growth expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real output without immediate inflation pressure, a mix that supports higher equity returns over the next month through better margins and cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns are not concentrated in periods where INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, or if the interaction effect is weaker than either single series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS reading above 4.5 percent marks a restrictive policy regime that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive during tightening episodes in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is positive while UNRATE is stable or falling, labor market momentum is translating into income growth and consumption support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns better than chance, or if labor-strength episodes are followed by negative returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS above its recent pace signals stronger consumer demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive in most episodes following the RSAFS growth trigger, or if the trigger does not improve predictive power relative to an unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST and INDPRO together indicate normalization of supply capacity and construction activity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint recovery signal does not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns during recovery periods, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling walk-forward tests.
